'''Mahwi Lihnn''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who was active during the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
In [[42 BBY/Legends|42 BBY]] Mahwi Lihnn was forced to leave her homeworld after killing a corrupt government official. She soon took up bounty hunting. During her ten years of bounty hunting before [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Mahwi Lihnn pursued [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]], [[Roon/Legends|Roon]], [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and other worlds.

In 32 BBY, Lihnn was hired by [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] to rein in and [[Skirmish on Coruscant (32 BBY)|bring back]] the "traitorous" [[Hath Monchar]]. Monchar had gone missing, much to the displeasure and slight worry of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. Sidious suspected that Monchar, who knew of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] upcoming [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|blockade of Naboo]], was treacherous and prepared to leak the sensitive plans.

Lihnn would track down Hath Monchar, but it would prove to be her last bounty hunt. Visiting [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for her first—and last—time, Lihnn searched Monchar's [[Kaldani Spires Residential Apartments|Kaldani Spires Residential apartment]] before tracking him to the [[Dewback Inn]]. Following the cloaked figure of [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], who was in turn following Monchar, Lihnn came to the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian's]] domicile only to find him already slain by Darth Maul. Maul, apprentice to Darth Sidious, had been dispatched by Sidious to kill Monchar and anybody he talked to. When the two hunters, knowing nothing of the other's parallel mission, encountered each other, Lihnn pulled her [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech]] [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|DL-44 heavy blaster pistols]] on Maul. She held the upper hand with Maul backed into a corner, but the Sith used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to distract Lihnn by hurling a fallen [[Gorth (Trandoshan)|bodyguard's]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] at her. Lihnn recovered quickly and fired at Maul as he ignited his [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]]. With the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] guiding his every move, Maul reflected multiple shots back at the bounty hunter. However, none of the blaster bolts could penetrate Lihnn's [[Shell spider silk|shell]] [[spider silk armor]]. Despite this, Lihnn soon grew desperate and prepared to fire an [[MM9 mini concussion rocket launcher/Legends|wrist-mounted rocket]]. Maul, recognizing the devastation such a blast could cause, fled the scene by slicing through the adjacent wall and down through the floor. Lihnn realized as the rocket fired that the Sith had escaped and attempted to jump back into the hallway. Too late, the blast of the explosion slammed Lihnn halfway through the far wall and messily ended the career—and life—of the female bounty hunter.

==Behind the scenes==
Mahwi Lihnn first appeared in the novel ''[[Darth Maul: Shadow Hunter]]''.

Mahwi Lihnn is voiced by [[Roger Jackson]] in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. She appears as a hero for the Trade Federation in the game's level editor. Like all the bounty hunter units of that faction, she is portrayed here as a white-skinned humanoid with a strange, high-pitched voice.
